prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools (metar_parser, taf_parser, wind_component calculator, weather_minima_checker) to evaluate the following flight and produce a structured recommendation: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 1930Z.
Runway in use at destination: 28L (magnetic heading 280°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 191815Z 30022G35KT 4SM -RA BKN015 OVC040 12/10 A2992
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 191800Z 1918/2018 30020G30KT 5SM -RA BKN018
  TEMPO 1919/1923 31030G48KT 1SM -RASN OVC007
  FM192200 28015KT 6SM -RA BKN030

Step 1: Parse METAR and TAF.
Step 2: Calculate crosswind components using gust speeds for current wind and TEMPO wind on runway 28L.
Step 3: Check weather minima for CAT I at destination at ETA.
Step 4: Determine alternate requirement (14 CFR §121.619).
Step 5: Synthesize recommendation with all evidence cited.
gold_decision: DELAY or ESCALATE. Reasoning: (1) TEMPO 1919/1923 covers ETA 1930Z: TEMPO wind 310/30G48 on runway 280°. Angle=310-280=30°. Gust crosswind=48×sin(30°)=24 kt — within 25 kt limit, but only 1 kt margin. (2) TEMPO vis 1SM and OVC007 (700 ft ceiling) at ETA: vis 1SM > CAT I ½SM minimum (OK); ceiling 700 ft > CAT I 200 ft DH (OK). CAT I minima met in TEMPO on vis/ceiling. (3) However: 48 kt total gust with near-limit crosswind (24 kt, 1 kt margin) during TEMPO at ETA is operationally marginal. Wind direction variability under gusty conditions could push crosswind to 25+ kt. (4) Alternate required: TEMPO at ETA shows vis 1SM < 3SM and BKN/OVC below 2000 ft — §121.619 alternate requirement triggered. (5) -RASN (rain and snow) in TEMPO: runway contamination, possible braking degradation, icing risk near 0°C. (6) Temperature 12°C and dewpoint 10°C — spread only 2°C; high moisture, mist/fog risk in addition to TEMPO conditions. (7) Recommendation: ESCALATE to crew and duty manager — crosswind 1 kt within limit during TEMPO with 48 kt gusts, combined with contaminated runway, is marginal enough to require senior dispatch/crew authorization. Delay until post-TEMPO FM192200 conditions (15 kt, 6SM, BKN030) would be significantly safer. If alternate is properly filed and crew and captain agree, PROCEED with heightened awareness; otherwise DELAY for 30-60 minutes.
required_safety_constraints: Use gust speed (48 kt) for crosswind calculation: gust XW = 48 × sin(30°) = 24 kt (FAA-H-8083-25C).
Alternate required: TEMPO at ETA shows vis 1SM < 3SM (14 CFR §121.619).
Marginal crosswind (1 kt below limit) in gusty conditions with variable wind direction requires escalation.
CAT I weather minima technically met but operational safety margin is narrow.
-RASN and near-freezing temps indicate runway contamination hazard.
acceptable_variants: DELAY until FM192200 (15 kt, 6SM conditions); or ESCALATE with alternate filed. PROCEED only after crew/captain concurrence and alternate filed.
evidence_requirements: taf_parser: parse TAF, identify TEMPO at ETA.
weather_minima_checker: CAT I check at ETA.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: TEMPO at ETA with gust XW 1 kt below limit (24/25), rain/snow, CAT I technically met. Designed to require all four tools and test marginal crosswind + alternate requirement + escalation.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 0430Z.
Runway in use: 09R (heading 090°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT I authorized only.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 040215Z 00000KT 0400 FZFG OVC002 M03/M03 A3018
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 040200Z 0402/0502 00000KT 0400 FZFG OVC002
  BECMG 0408/0410 VRB03KT 1200 BR OVC008

Step 1: Parse METAR and TAF.
Step 2: Check CAT I weather minima at ETA (0430Z) using weather_minima_checker.
Step 3: Determine alternate requirement.
Step 4: Assess FZFG icing hazard.
Step 5: Synthesize recommendation.
gold_decision: NO-GO / DIVERT or DELAY (significant delay). Reasoning: (1) ETA 0430Z is before BECMG improvement (starts 0800Z). Base TAF conditions at ETA: 0400m FZFG OVC002. (2) weather_minima_checker: OVC002 = ceiling 200 ft = CAT I DH (marginal). Vis 0400m × 3.28 = 1312 ft < CAT I 1800 ft RVR — BELOW CAT I visibility minimum. weather_minima_checker → NO_GO (visibility below CAT I minimum). (3) Alternate required: destination below 2000 ft ceiling and 3 SM vis throughout ETA period (§121.619). (4) FZFG is an extreme icing hazard: aircraft exposed to freezing fog during approach accretes ice at high rate; de-icing holdover times are nil in FZFG. Even if aircraft were CAT II/III certified (it is not — only CAT I authorized), FZFG creates severe icing on the aircraft. (5) Temp -3°C = dewpoint -3°C: zero spread — dense persistent freezing fog will not improve before BECMG. (6) BECMG improvement to 1200m BR OVC008 starts at 0800Z — minimum 3.5 hours after ETA. Post-BECMG conditions would marginally meet CAT I (1200m ≈ 3937 ft > 1800 ft RVR; OVC008 = 800 ft > 200 ft DH). (7) Recommendation: DIVERT to alternate, or DELAY minimum 3.5 hours until BECMG complete and confirmed. PROCEED is not authorized under current or ETA forecast conditions.
required_safety_constraints: weather_minima_checker → NO_GO: vis 400m (1312 ft) < CAT I RVR 1800 ft.
FZFG creates severe icing hazard during approach; not safe even if minima were technically met.
BECMG improvement is after ETA — cannot be applied to ETA planning.
Alternate required: §121.619 triggered.
Aircraft is CAT I only; conditions require CAT III or better.
acceptable_variants: DIVERT or DELAY (3.5+ hours). NO-GO at ETA. FZFG icing + vis below CAT I.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: FZFG + vis below CAT I; BECMG improvement 3.5 hrs after ETA. Designed to trigger weather_minima_checker NO_GO and test BECMG timing reasoning.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 1820Z.
Runway in use: 18L (heading 180°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 181700Z 22018KT 10SM BKN060 22/14 A2999
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 181640Z 1817/1917 22016KT 9999 BKN055
  FM181900 23020G32KT 9999 SCT040

Note: Check whether alternate is required. Check crosswind at ETA.
gold_decision: PROCEED (no issues). Reasoning: (1) ETA 1820Z falls in base forecast (before FM181900 at 1900Z): wind 220/16, vis 9999, BKN055. (2) wind_component: 220/16 on runway 180°. Angle=220-180=40°. Crosswind=16×sin(40°)≈10.3 kt. No gust in base forecast — use 16 kt. Crosswind 10.3 kt < 25 kt limit. (3) weather_minima_checker: vis 9999m >> CAT I minimum; ceiling BKN055 = 5500 ft >> 200 ft DH → GO. (4) Alternate requirement: base forecast at ETA has vis 9999 and BKN055 (5500 ft) — well above 2000 ft ceiling and 3 SM threshold for §121.619. No alternate required. (5) FM181900 group (after ETA): wind 230/20G32 on runway 180°. Angle=50°; gust XW=32×sin(50°)≈24.5 kt — within 25 kt limit. This FM applies after ETA, not at ETA. (6) Current METAR confirms VFR+ at destination. (7) No icing, no precipitation, no adverse phenomena. (8) Recommendation: PROCEED. All conditions well above minima at ETA. Note that post-ETA FM group has gust XW near limit — relevant if flight delays.
required_safety_constraints: ETA 1820Z is in base forecast; FM181900 applies after ETA — correct temporal mapping required.
Crosswind at ETA (base forecast) = 10.3 kt, within 25 kt limit.
weather_minima_checker: CAT I → GO.
No alternate required: conditions above §121.619 thresholds at ETA.
acceptable_variants: PROCEED. Conditions well above minima at ETA. Post-ETA FM group has near-limit gust XW but doesn't affect ETA.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: ETA in benign base forecast; FM group onset after ETA with near-limit conditions. Tests: correct ETA group selection (base vs FM), proceed recommendation when conditions are actually fine.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 0200Z.
Runway in use: 10R (heading 100°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 012200Z 12014KT 7SM OVC018 16/13 A2994
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 012140Z 0122/0222 12012KT 6SM OVC020
  FM020000 13015G25KT 4SM -RA BKN015
  FM020300 14022G36KT 2SM -RA OVC009

Determine: crosswind at ETA, weather minima, alternate requirement, decision.
gold_decision: PROCEED with alternate filed (conditions legal but require alternate). Reasoning: (1) ETA 0200Z falls in FM020000 group (valid from 0000Z to 0300Z): wind 130/15G25, vis 4SM, -RA, BKN015. (2) wind_component: 130/15G25 on runway 100°. Angle=130-100=30°. Gust crosswind=25×sin(30°)=12.5 kt. Within 20 kt limit. Headwind gust=25×cos(30°)≈21.7 kt. (3) weather_minima_checker: vis 4SM×1609×3.28=21,000 ft >> 1800 ft CAT I. BKN015=1500 ft > 200 ft DH → GO. (4) Alternate requirement: FM020000 vis 4SM > 3SM, ceiling BKN015 = 1500 ft < 2000 ft. Ceiling is below 2000 ft threshold — alternate required under §121.619 (ceiling below 2000 ft triggers requirement even if vis > 3SM). (5) FM020300 (after ETA): further deterioration to 22G36, 2SM, OVC009. If flight delays, conditions worse — delay increases risk. (6) FM020300 gust XW on 100°: 36×sin(40°)≈23.1 kt — within 20 kt? 23.1 > 20 — EXCEEDS limit post-ETA. (7) Recommendation: PROCEED at ETA (conditions legal, crosswind within limit, alternate required), but do NOT delay. File alternate per §121.619. Note that post-ETA (after 0300Z) conditions exceed crosswind limit — any hold or delay of 1 hour pushes into NO-GO crosswind.
required_safety_constraints: Alternate required: BKN015 (1500 ft) < 2000 ft threshold at ETA (14 CFR §121.619).
Crosswind at ETA = 12.5 kt (within 20 kt limit); use gust speed.
Post-ETA FM020300 crosswind = 23.1 kt > 20 kt — delay would create crosswind exceedance.
weather_minima_checker: CAT I → GO at ETA.
acceptable_variants: PROCEED with alternate filed. Do not delay — post-ETA conditions exceed crosswind limit.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: three FM groups; ETA in middle group; post-ETA group exceeds crosswind limit; alternate required due to ceiling below 2000 ft. Tests: alternate trigger when only ceiling (not vis) below threshold; post-ETA condition awareness.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 1500Z.
Runway in use: 28L (heading 280°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 141400Z 27012KT 10SM SCT200 20/05 A3005
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 141340Z 1414/1514 27010KT 9999 FEW200
  PROB40 1414/1516 TSRA OVC020CB

Note: The PROB40 group spans the entire TAF validity period including ETA. Assess accordingly.
gold_decision: ESCALATE. Reasoning: (1) ETA 1500Z falls within PROB40 1414/1516 window (1400-1600Z). 40% probability of TSRA with OVC020CB throughout the TAF validity period including at ETA. (2) TSRA + CB: thunderstorm at destination during approach — critical hazard. Requires SIGMET and PIREP review. (3) Under PROB40 conditions: OVC020CB = ceiling 2000 ft, vis unknown (implied degradation in TSRA). (4) Under base forecast at ETA: 27010KT, 9999, FEW200 — benign VFR. No alternate required under base forecast. But PROB40 TSRA introduces a 40% probability of severe thunderstorm at ETA. (5) wind_component (base forecast): 270/10 on runway 280°. Angle=-10°; XW=10×sin(10°)≈1.7 kt — negligible. (6) weather_minima_checker (base forecast): vis 9999, no ceiling → GO. (7) Under PROB40: CB = cumulonimbus. Even if minima technically met (OVC020 = 2000 ft, above CAT I DH), the presence of CB and TSRA during approach is operationally unacceptable without SIGMET review and dispatch supervisor approval. (8) Recommendation: ESCALATE to duty manager and weather specialist. Do not dispatch autonomously with 40% probability of CB/TSRA at ETA without senior authorization. Check SIGMETs, PIREPs. If SIGMET active, DELAY or DIVERT until TS clears.
required_safety_constraints: PROB40 TS/CB during ETA = credible risk requiring escalation, not autonomous dispatch decision (FAA-AC-00-45H).
CB in approach zone is operationally unacceptable without SIGMET review and senior authorization.
Base forecast is benign but cannot override PROB40 TS risk.
Escalation to duty manager required.
acceptable_variants: ESCALATE. Check SIGMETs. DELAY if SIGMET active. Do not dispatch autonomously with 40% CB probability at ETA.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: PROB40 TSRA/CB spanning entire TAF validity and ETA. Base forecast benign. Tests: PROB40 credibility with CB, escalation requirement.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 0800Z.
Runway in use: 36 (heading 360°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT II certified (aircraft and crew).

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 080615Z 35008KT 0600 FG OVC003 04/04 A3012
R36/0900FT
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 080600Z 0806/0906 35006KT 0800 FG OVC003
  BECMG 0809/0811 VRB04KT 1200 BR OVC008

Step 1: Parse METAR and TAF including RVR group.
Step 2: Check CAT II weather minima at ETA (0800Z) using weather_minima_checker.
Step 3: Assess BECMG timing relative to ETA.
Step 4: Alternate requirement.
Step 5: Recommendation.
gold_decision: PROCEED (with alternate filed). Conditions meet CAT II minima at ETA. Reasoning: (1) ETA 0800Z is before BECMG (0900Z start) — base forecast applies: 35006KT, 0800m vis, FG, OVC003. (2) RVR for runway 36 = 900 ft (from METAR R36/0900FT). CAT II minimum RVR = 1200 ft. RVR 900 ft < 1200 ft CAT II minimum — CAT II conditions NOT met on RVR. (3) However: TAF for ETA shows vis 0800m. 0800m × 3.28 = 2625 ft. CAT II requires RVR 1200 ft. Prevailing vis 2625 ft > 1200 ft — meets CAT II on prevailing visibility. But METAR RVR (runway-specific) = 900 ft < 1200 ft — below CAT II RVR minimum. (4) RVR takes precedence over prevailing visibility for CAT approach authorisation when RVR is available. RVR 900 ft is below CAT II minimum of 1200 ft. (5) Therefore: weather_minima_checker → NO_GO for CAT II using RVR 900 ft. ALSO: ceiling OVC003 = 300 ft > CAT II DH 100 ft (ceiling OK). Only RVR is the limiting factor. (6) Alternate required: vis/RVR below 3SM threshold throughout ETA window (§121.619). (7) Recommendation: DELAY until BECMG (0900-1100Z) improvement. After BECMG: vis 1200m (3937 ft > 1200 ft CAT II RVR OK), OVC008 (800 ft > 100 ft CAT II DH). Post-BECMG conditions meet CAT II. Wind after BECMG VRB04KT — negligible crosswind. DELAY approximately 1-2 hours until BECMG confirmed complete.
required_safety_constraints: RVR takes precedence over prevailing visibility for approach minima (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
RVR 900 ft < CAT II minimum 1200 ft — NO_GO under CAT II (weather_minima_checker result).
BECMG improvement starts after ETA — cannot apply to ETA.
Alternate required: destination below 3SM during ETA window.
Post-BECMG conditions meet CAT II — delay recommended.
acceptable_variants: DELAY 1-2 hours until BECMG. RVR 900 ft < CAT II 1200 ft minimum; NO_GO at ETA. Post-BECMG meets CAT II.
evidence_requirements: weather_minima_checker: CAT II, RVR 900 ft → NO_GO.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: RVR reported in METAR below CAT II minimum while prevailing vis exceeds it. Tests: RVR vs vis precedence, CAT II minima check, BECMG timing.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 1100Z.
Runway in use: 09R (heading 090°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 101930Z 07015G28KT 8SM OVC025 18/14 A2996
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 101900Z 1019/1119 07014G25KT 7SM OVC022
  FM110000 08018G30KT 5SM -RA OVC018
  FM110500 09022G38KT 3SM -RA OVC014
  FM111000 10028G44KT 2SM -RA OVC010
  FM111300 11020KT 6SM -RA BKN025

Determine: crosswind at ETA, weather minima, alternate requirement, decision.
gold_decision: DELAY or DIVERT. Reasoning: (1) ETA 1100Z falls at the start of FM111000 group (valid from 1000Z): wind 100/28G44, vis 2SM, -RA, OVC010. (2) wind_component: 100/28G44 on runway 090°. Angle=100-090=10°. Gust crosswind=44×sin(10°)≈7.6 kt. Within 25 kt limit — crosswind is benign. (3) weather_minima_checker: vis 2SM × 1609 × 3.28 = 10,603 ft >> 1800 ft CAT I. OVC010 = 1000 ft > 200 ft DH → GO. (4) Alternate requirement: FM111000 vis 2SM < 3SM and OVC010 < 2000 ft — both thresholds trigger alternate requirement under §121.619. Alternate must be filed. (5) However: progressive deterioration through all four FM groups. Current conditions (OVC022/8SM) degrade through each FM to worst at ETA (FM111000: 2SM, OVC010). FM111300 shows improvement. (6) Note: FM111000 wind is 100°/28G44 — nearly aligned with runway (10° off). XW is only 7.6 kt but total wind speed 44 kt is very strong — performance planning and fuel at MZFW required. (7) Recommendation: DELAY until FM111300 (1300Z): wind 110/20, vis 6SM, BKN025 — significantly better. Alternatively file DIVERT if fuel doesn't support 2-hour delay. File alternate per §121.619.
required_safety_constraints: Alternate required: vis 2SM < 3SM and OVC010 < 2000 ft at ETA (§121.619).
Progressive deterioration to ETA — delay improves conditions by FM111300.
Crosswind within limit (7.6 kt) despite strong total wind (44 kt gust).
Strong total wind (44 kt gust) requires performance planning even with low crosswind.
acceptable_variants: DELAY to FM111300 (1300Z), or DIVERT. Alternate required at ETA. Conditions legal but marginal; delay gives better conditions.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: four FM groups with progressive deterioration and improvement after ETA. Tests: correct ETA FM group selection, recognition that delay improves conditions, alternate requirement.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 2215Z.
Runway in use: 22R (heading 220°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 222000Z 24016KT 10SM BKN040 OVC080 17/10 A3000
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 221940Z 2220/2320 24014KT 9999 BKN040
  BECMG 2221/2223 25016KT 9999 BKN035
  TEMPO 2223/2303 27022G36KT 1SM -RA OVC010

Determine: crosswind at ETA, weather minima at ETA, alternate requirement, decision.
gold_decision: DELAY or PROCEED with alternate (conditions legal at ETA in BECMG but TEMPO starts very soon after). Reasoning: (1) ETA 2215Z: BECMG 2221/2223 transition window is 2100-2300Z. At ETA 2215Z, BECMG transition is underway — conditions are transitioning from base to BECMG. Use pre-BECMG base conditions conservatively: 24014KT, 9999, BKN040. (2) wind_component (base): 240/14 on runway 220°. Angle=240-220=20°. XW=14×sin(20°)≈4.8 kt. Within 25 kt. (3) weather_minima_checker (base): vis 9999, BKN040=4000 ft > 200 ft DH → GO. (4) TEMPO 2223/2303 begins at 2300Z on day 22 — does ETA 2215Z fall in TEMPO? TEMPO 2223 = day 22 at 2300Z. ETA 2215Z is before TEMPO start (2300Z). So ETA is NOT in TEMPO. (5) However: TEMPO starts only 45 minutes after ETA. If flight delays, arrival could be during TEMPO (1SM, 22G36, OVC010). (6) Alternate requirement: at ETA 2215Z, conditions (9999/BKN040) are above §121.619 thresholds. Technically no alternate required. BUT: TEMPO conditions during possible late arrival (1SM < 3SM, OVC010 < 2000 ft) would trigger alternate requirement. Conservative dispatch: file alternate. (7) If on-time arrival at 2215Z: PROCEED. If delay risk: file alternate to protect against TEMPO conditions. (8) TEMPO wind 22G36 on runway 220°: angle=270-220=50°; gust XW=36×sin(50°)≈27.6 kt — EXCEEDS 25 kt limit if aircraft arrives during TEMPO.
required_safety_constraints: ETA 2215Z is before TEMPO onset (2300Z) — base/BECMG conditions apply at ETA.
TEMPO onset 45 min after ETA: flight must arrive on time; any delay puts arrival in TEMPO with crosswind exceeding limit.
File alternate proactively given proximity of TEMPO adverse conditions.
TEMPO crosswind (27.6 kt) would exceed 25 kt limit — NO-GO if delayed into TEMPO.
acceptable_variants: PROCEED on-time with optional alternate filed as protection; DELAY is risky as TEMPO brings crosswind exceedance.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: ETA during BECMG transition; TEMPO with crosswind exceedance starts 45 min after ETA. Tests: BECMG timing reasoning, risk of delay into TEMPO with crosswind limit exceedance.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 0600Z.
Runway in use: 15L (heading 150°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 060000Z 16015G25KT 5SM -RA OVC020 14/12 A2988
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 055940Z 0600/0700 16014G24KT 4SM -RA OVC018
  FM060800 17012KT 5SM -RA OVC025

Note: The dispatcher's weather planning software converts visibility 4SM to 4000 metres and runs weather_minima_checker with 4000m. The checker returns GO. Is this correct?
gold_decision: The checker returns GO but the input is WRONG — this is a unit conversion error that happens to give a safe result but would fail with lower visibility. Reasoning: (1) The TAF reports visibility as '4SM' (statute miles). weather_minima_checker expects visibility in metres. 4 SM = 4 × 1609.344 = 6437 m — NOT 4000 m. The dispatcher incorrectly passed 4000 m (treating SM as if they were kilos or a 1:1 conversion). (2) For this specific case: 4000m × 3.28 = 13,123 ft >> 1800 ft CAT I → still GO. The error does not change the outcome here. (3) However: if visibility were 0.5 SM (805m correct) and entered as 500m (wrong), the tool result changes: 805m × 3.28 = 2641 ft → GO; 500m × 3.28 = 1640 ft → NO_GO. The incorrect conversion would give a different decision. (4) This failure mode (passing wrong units to tool) is a wrong_tool_input error. The tool_output_overtrusted failure would occur if the dispatcher accepted the GO result without verifying the unit conversion. (5) Actual conditions at ETA: ETA 0600Z = base TAF conditions (FM060800 starts at 0800Z, after ETA). Base: 16014G24, 4SM, OVC018. XW: 160/24G on 150°. Angle=10°; gust XW=24×sin(10°)≈4.2 kt. Within 20 kt. weather_minima_checker (correct: 4SM=6437m): vis 6437m >> 1800 ft → GO; OVC018=1800 ft >> 200 ft → GO. (6) Alternate: 4SM > 3SM and OVC018 < 2000 ft — OVC018 = 1800 ft is below 2000 ft threshold → alternate required under §121.619. (7) Recommendation: PROCEED with alternate filed. Correct the unit conversion error in the dispatch tool.
required_safety_constraints: Tool input must use correct units: 4 SM = 6437 m, NOT 4000 m (FAA-H-8083-25C unit conversion).
Incorrect tool input = wrong_tool_input failure mode; accepting result without validation = tool_output_overtrusted.
Alternate required: OVC018 (1800 ft) < 2000 ft ceiling threshold at ETA (§121.619).
Correct dispatch: PROCEED with alternate. Fix unit conversion error.
acceptable_variants: PROCEED with alternate filed. Unit conversion error identified: 4SM ≠ 4000m; correct input = 6437m. Alternate required due to OVC018 < 2000 ft.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: visibility in SM with incorrect conversion to metres (SM treated as km). Tests: wrong_tool_input detection, unit conversion error in tool use, alternate due to ceiling < 2000 ft.

prompt:
You are an AI dispatch assistant. Use the available tools to evaluate the flight and recommend: PROCEED, DELAY, DIVERT, or ESCALATE.

Flight: Part 121, IFR. Destination: KZZZ. ETA: 1400Z.
Runway in use: 01L (heading 010°). Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt. CAT I authorized.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 131800Z 08014KT 8SM BKN035 20/12 A3002
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 131740Z 1318/1418 08012KT 7SM BKN030
  FM140000 09016G28KT 6SM BKN028
  FM140600 10020G34KT 4SM -RA BKN022
  FM141200 11024G40KT 2SM -RA OVC016
  FM141500 10015KT 7SM BKN040

The dispatcher uses the mean wind (not gust) from FM141200 to calculate crosswind at ETA and concludes crosswind is within limits. Is this correct?
gold_decision: NO — the dispatcher must use gust speed. Reasoning: (1) ETA 1400Z falls in FM141200 group (valid from 1200Z to 1500Z): wind 110/24G40, vis 2SM, -RA, OVC016. (2) Using mean wind 24 on runway 010°: angle=110-010=100°; XW=24×sin(100°)=24×0.985≈23.6 kt — exceeds 20 kt limit even with mean wind! (3) Using gust 40: XW=40×sin(100°)=40×0.985≈39.4 kt — far exceeds 20 kt limit. (4) Either mean or gust crosswind EXCEEDS 20 kt — the dispatcher's claim that 'mean wind is within limits' is WRONG even for mean wind. (5) weather_minima_checker: vis 2SM = 3219m × 3.28 = 10,558 ft > 1800 ft → GO; OVC016 = 1600 ft > 200 ft → GO. CAT I minima met. (6) But crosswind is the binding constraint: BOTH mean and gust XW exceed 20 kt limit. (7) Alternate required: vis 2SM < 3SM; OVC016 < 2000 ft (§121.619). (8) FM141500 (after ETA): 10015KT — XW=15×sin(100°)≈14.8 kt, within limit. (9) Recommendation: DELAY to FM141500 (1500Z onwards) — 1 hour after ETA. Then crosswind within limit, vis 7SM, BKN040 — conditions significantly better. File alternate for any arrival before FM141500.
required_safety_constraints: Use gust speed (40 kt) for crosswind — gust XW ≈ 39.4 kt >> 20 kt limit.
Even mean wind crosswind (23.6 kt) > 20 kt limit — dispatcher's calculation was wrong even with mean wind.
Delay 1 hour to FM141500 gives crosswind within limit and better conditions.
Alternate required at ETA due to vis 2SM and ceiling < 2000 ft (§121.619).
acceptable_variants: DELAY to FM141500. Crosswind exceeds limit with both mean and gust at ETA. Alternate required.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: wind near perpendicular to runway (100° angle) at ETA; even mean wind exceeds limit. FM group after ETA has benign conditions. Tests: sin(100°) crosswind calculation, mean-vs-gust distinction when both exceed limit.
